evidence.scientific_clarity: Pipeline flow (Input→Retriever→KB→DiffusionDecoder→Prior→Output) is readable with clear solid arrows and dashed group boundaries; no feedback/dashed control arrows despite brief requesting them.
evidence.typography: Script uses DejaVu Sans (matplotlib default) throughout; no pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype=0 set — PDF embeds Type 3 bitmapped fonts (not editable TrueType/Type1), failing editability requirement.
evidence.palette: Multi-hue pastel fills with distinct border colors; scheme is largely distinguishable, but no explicit colorblind-safety check (e.g. blue/orange group boundaries are CB-safe, purple datastore may be marginal); green used for output (directional connotation) is acceptable in this context.
evidence.layout_density: Figure is well-proportioned with nodes spread horizontally; group bounding boxes use most of the canvas; small gap between 'Output' node and right edge; no overlapping labels observed.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption accurately describes what is shown and matches the figure; legend is placed bottom-right without overlap; missing: brief asked for dashed control/feedback arrows but none were drawn — a reviewer would note this omission.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF is real vector (not rasterized), but fonts are Type 3 (bitmapped subsets) because pdf.fonttype=42 was never set in the script; text is not selectable/editable as TrueType in Illustrator/Inkscape.
evidence.file_format: All four required files exist and are non-trivial: figure.pdf (20 kB), figure.png (65 kB), figure.svg (39 kB), caption.tex (511 B), gen_figure.py (5.8 kB).
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names both groups and all modules, describes solid-arrow data flow, and traces the processing path; however it contains no numerical claim or quantitative finding — appropriate for an architecture diagram but slightly below top marks.

overall_strengths: Clean, readable architecture diagram with correct directional flow arrows and visually distinct group bounding boxes matching the brief's Retrieval/Generation groupings.
All required output files (PDF, PNG, SVG, caption.tex, gen_figure.py) are present and non-trivial; caption faithfully describes the figure.
overall_weaknesses: Script does not set pdf.fonttype=42 (or svg.fonttype=0), causing PDF to embed Type 3 bitmapped fonts instead of editable TrueType — text cannot be selected or edited in vector tools.
Brief explicitly requested dashed arrows for control/feedback paths, but the generated figure uses only solid arrows; this functional requirement is entirely missing.
